title: Allergy list update
specialty: Preventive care / screening / counseling / administrative documentation

examination.text: Supports documenting history, examination, timing after exposure, erythema, urticaria, angioedema, respiratory or cardiovascular features, macules, papules, fixed eruption, fever, lymphadenopathy, mucosal erosion, vesicles, blistering, detachment, pustules, photosensitivity, and vasculitic features without asserting a diagnosis.
examination.rationale: Supports medicine, reaction-pattern and timing assessment for allergy list update; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Supports documenting history, examination, timing after exposure, erythema, urticaria, angioedema, respiratory or cardiovascular features, macules, papules, fixed eruption, fever, lymphadenopathy, mucosal erosion, vesicles, blistering, detachment, pustules, photosensitivity, and vasculitic features without asserting a diagnosis.

source.title: Drug allergy: diagnosis and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendation 1.1.1 and Tables 1–3 — assessment, reaction patterns, and timing

investigations.text: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.
investigations.rationale: Supports structured recording and sharing of drug-allergy information for allergy list update; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Supports structured recording of drug name, reaction signs, symptoms, severity, date and time, indication, number of doses or days before onset, route, status, and specialist-investigation results already available.

source.title: Drug allergy: diagnosis and management — Recommendations

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.9 — recording and sharing drug-allergy information

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Substance, reaction, timing, severity, route, exposure count, treatment, outcome, confirmation status, record changes and clinician plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for allergy list update.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
